display.purpose: Popup/modal titles — the event name at the top of the event popup.

body.purpose: Default UI text — labels, descriptions, form text, base html/body size.

label.purpose: Uppercase structural chrome — sidebar section headers.

numeric.purpose: Clock times, day numbers, durations — anything fundamentally a number.

shadows.name: popup
shadows.purpose: The event detail popup — moderate lift with a soft outer shadow and a tighter contact shadow.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Full modals (event form, search) that block the whole screen.

shadows.name: dragging
shadows.purpose: An event card mid-drag — the strongest shadow in the system.

shadows.name: chrome
shadows.purpose: Hairline lift on small floating chrome like a raised nav button.

motion.name: state-change
motion.purpose: Default transition for hover/focus/opacity state changes across buttons, inputs, and nav rows.

motion.name: quick-shift
motion.purpose: Faster transition for small chrome elements (dots, icons, toggles).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one committing action per view — Save, Log in.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: var(--accent, #4f7ef8); color: #ffffff; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; padding: 9px 16px; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius, 6px); cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.88; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #4f7ef8); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.45; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: var(--text-2, #9095a8); font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; padding: 9px 16px; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius, 6px); cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--bg-hover, #23262f); color: var(--text, #e8eaf0); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #4f7ef8); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: Circular chrome button — close, more-options, small toolbar actions.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-icon" aria-label="Close"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none"><path d="M4 4L12 12M12 4L4 12" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-btn-icon { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; background: transparent; color: var(--text-2, #9095a8); border: none; border-radius: 50%; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.1s ease, color 0.1s ease; } .ds-btn-icon:hover { background: var(--bg-hover, #23262f); color: var(--text, #e8eaf0); } .ds-btn-icon:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #4f7ef8); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field — event title, search box, login fields.
components.css: .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; background: var(--bg-3, #1e2028); color: var(--text, #e8eaf0); font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; padding: 9px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--border, #2a2d38); border-radius: var(--radius, 6px); transition: border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-3, #5c6070); } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--accent, #4f7ef8); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: A row in the sidebar — view switcher or calendar toggle.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; width: 100%; padding: 8px 12px; background: transparent; color: var(--text-2, #9095a8); font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius, 6px); cursor: pointer; text-align: left; transition: background 0.1s ease, color 0.1s ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--bg-hover, #23262f); color: var(--text, #e8eaf0); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: rgba(79, 126, 248, 0.15); color: var(--accent, #4f7ef8); } .ds-nav-dot { width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 50%; background: var(--accent, #4f7ef8); flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Event Chip
components.description: A scheduled event on the calendar grid, color-coded by its source calendar (functional, not decorative — see The Calendar-Owns-Its-Color Rule).
components.css: .ds-event-chip { display: block; padding: 4px 8px; background: rgba(79, 126, 248, 0.14); color: var(--text, #e8eaf0); font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; border-left: 2.5px solid var(--accent, #4f7ef8); border-radius: var(--radius, 6px); }

narrative.overview: Termina is a dark, quiet workspace with one deliberate point of warmth. The system is built for a single owner doing a fast glance-and-plan loop against their own Nextcloud calendar — every surface stays low-contrast and out of the way (ink-black backgrounds, graphite panels, restrained slate text) so the schedule itself is the only thing competing for attention. Signal Blue is the one color allowed to carry emotional weight: it marks today, the accent action, the thing currently in focus. It is not decoration, it is the system's single named signal in an otherwise hushed field. This is explicitly not a SaaS admin dashboard, and not a cold developer terminal either. PRODUCT.md calls for "warm and personal, not clinical" — Termina answers that by staying structurally precise (tight radii, mono numerals, minimal chrome) while keeping the emotional register calm and human at the edges: soft hover states, a gentle blue wash rather than hard highlights, generous line-height on body text. It should feel like checking a well-kept desk calendar in low evening light, not opening an ops console. It should also stay legible to the muscle memory of Google Calendar / Apple Calendar — familiar grid, familiar event chips, familiar color-coding — because relearning a calendar is friction the owner never asked for.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black neutral field (ink-black → panel-charcoal → graphite-surface) with almost no saturation outside the accent.
Exactly one system accent (Signal Blue, #4f7ef8) used sparingly for focus, action, and "today."
Calendar-native color-coding on events is data-driven, not part of the palette — each Nextcloud calendar owns its own hue.
DM Sans for everything conversational, DM Mono for anything numeric (times, dates, durations).
Flat by default; elevation only appears on things that are literally floating above the grid (popups, modals, a dragged event).

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue is the only saturated color the design system itself contributes. If a screen needs a second "important" color, that's a sign something should be de-emphasized instead, not a cue to add a second accent.

rules.name: The Calendar-Owns-Its-Color Rule
rules.body: Event and calendar-list colors come from the user's Nextcloud calendars, not from this palette. They render as a small dot next to the calendar name and as a left edge on event chips/cards (2–3px), mirroring Google/Apple Calendar. This is functional data encoding, not a decorative accent border.

rules.name: The Numbers-Are-Mono Rule
rules.body: Anything that is a clock time, date, or duration renders in DM Mono, even inline in otherwise-Sans text.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadows are prohibited on anything still part of the normal page flow (cells, chips, sidebar rows, static cards). They're reserved entirely for things temporarily above the grid: popups, modals, drag state.

dos: Do keep Signal Blue (#4f7ef8) as the only system-contributed accent — reserve it for today, focus, links, and the single primary action per view.
Do let per-calendar CalDAV colors own event/calendar-list color; treat them as data, not palette.
Do use DM Mono for any clock time, date, or duration, even inline in Sans text.
Do keep the calendar grid, sidebar rows, and event chips flat — shadows are reserved for popups, modals, and drag state only.
Do default to Google/Apple Calendar-familiar interaction patterns for the core scheduling grid.
Do push warmth through hover states, the accent, and motion — components can stay structurally precise and quiet without needing softer shapes to feel human.
donts: Don't add a second saturated system accent alongside Signal Blue.
Don't add decorative border-left/border-right accents anywhere except the functional per-calendar event-color edge already documented.
Don't reach for a generic SaaS admin dashboard look — identical card grids, hero-metric tiles, tiny uppercase eyebrow labels above every section.
Don't let the system drift toward cold "dev tool" starkness — if a new screen feels like an admin panel or a terminal rather than a calendar, that's a regression.
Don't invent new calendar-grid interaction patterns that require relearning; match Google/Apple Calendar conventions unless there's a concrete reason to diverge.
Don't use glow/ring focus effects — focus and hover feedback in this system is color-shift and opacity only.
